mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns within one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and produces positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 65% of co-expansion periods during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating short-term positive market momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds 2% annually, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0) outside the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stationary federal funds rate creates a real rate compression that forces equity valuation multiples to contract, resulting in negative forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% monthly while FEDFUNDS changes < 0.05%
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when real rates decline due to inflation surprises, or the interaction term yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and supports corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.015 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 1% annualized triggers immediate risk-off positioning and margin calls, causing negative equity returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of GDP contraction quarters, or the threshold signal fails to generate a statistically significant directional edge (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling consumer prices indicates deflationary supply shocks that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 0.5% monthly while CPIAUCSL declines > 0.2% monthly
failure_condition: Corporate margins expand during deflationary supply shocks, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of divergence episodes during walk-forward validation.
